title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: Reviewer requests: (1) remove the leftover `Reviewer: please verify.` comment in `buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy`, (2) surface an error/log instead of silently falling back to cluster-local URL in `keystoneStatusEndpoint` when `spec.gateway` is set but `hostname` is empty, (3) normalize/validate `gw.Path` in `reconcile_httproute.go` to ensure a leading `/`, and (4) make the Gateway-namespace ingress peer in `reconcile_networkpolicy.go` opt-in rather than automatic to avoid weakening intended isolation.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In `buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy` the inline comment still contains `Reviewer: please verify.`; consider removing this leftover note now that the behavior is codified and covered by tests.
- In `keystoneStatusEndpoint`, when `spec.gateway` is non-nil but `hostname` is empty you silently fall back to the cluster-local URL; if this situation should never occur post-admission, it might be safer to log or surface this as a validation/error to avoid misconfigurations being masked.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy` the inline comment still contains `Reviewer: please verify.`; consider removing this leftover note now that the behavior is codified and covered by tests.
- In `keystoneStatusEndpoint`, when `spec.gateway` is non-nil but `hostname` is empty you silently fall back to the cluster-local URL; if this situation should never occur post-admission, it might be safer to log or surface this as a validation/error to avoid misconfigurations being masked.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute.go" line_range="138-140" />
<code_context>
+		parentRef.SectionName = ptr.To(gatewayv1.SectionName(gw.ParentRef.SectionName))
+	}
+
+	path := gw.Path
+	if path == "" {
+		path = defaultHTTPRoutePath
+	}
+
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (bug_risk):** Gateway path is used verbatim without basic validation or normalization (e.g. leading slash), which may lead to invalid HTTPRoute specs.

Because `gw.Path` is passed directly into `HTTPPathMatch.Value`, a value without a leading `/` or in an invalid format can cause the generated HTTPRoute to be rejected. Consider enforcing a leading `/` (and other basic format checks) in the webhook or normalizing here (e.g. prepend `/` when missing) and rejecting clearly invalid values so it truly behaves as a URL path prefix.

Suggested implementation:

```golang
	if gw.ParentRef.SectionName != "" {
		parentRef.SectionName = ptr.To(gatewayv1.SectionName(gw.ParentRef.SectionName))
	}

	path := gw.Path
	if path == "" {
		path = defaultHTTPRoutePath
	}
	if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "/") {
		path = "/" + path
	}

package controller

import (
	"context"

```

```golang
	"fmt"

	"strings"

	apiequality "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality"

```

1. Find all usages where `gw.Path` is passed into the HTTPRoute (e.g. in `HTTPPathMatch.Value`) and replace them with the normalized `path` variable introduced above.
   For example, change:
   `Value: ptr.To(gw.Path),`
   to:
   `Value: ptr.To(path),`
2. If there are any other places in this reconcile function where `gw.Path` is used to construct HTTPRoute rules or matches, update those to use the normalized `path` variable as well, to keep behavior consistent.
</issue_to_address>

### Comment 2
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go" line_range="115-103" />
<code_context>
+	// ParentRef lookup semantics. When spec.networkPolicy is nil we take the
+	// delete path above — no extra rule is needed. Reviewer: please verify.
+	// (CC-0065, REQ-008)
+	if keystone.Spec.Gateway != nil {
+		gatewayNS := keystone.Spec.Gateway.ParentRef.Namespace
+		if gatewayNS == "" {
+			gatewayNS = keystone.Namespace
+		}
+		peers = append(peers, networkingv1.NetworkPolicyPeer{
+			NamespaceSelector: &metav1.LabelSelector{
+				MatchLabels: map[string]string{
+					"kubernetes.io/metadata.name": gatewayNS,
+				},
+			},
+		})
+	}
+
 	port5000 := intstr.FromInt32(5000)
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**🚨 question (security):** Automatically allowing all pods in the Gateway namespace may weaken the intended isolation expressed by spec.networkPolicy.

When `spec.gateway` is set, this always adds an ingress peer for the entire Gateway namespace, even if `spec.networkPolicy` is intended to be more restrictive. In shared Gateway namespaces, this can grant broader access than the CR author expects. Consider guarding this with an explicit opt-in (e.g. a field under `spec.networkPolicy` or `spec.gateway`), or only adding this peer when no other ingress peers are defined, so that the CR’s `networkPolicy` remains the complete source of truth for ingress policy.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

	"fmt"

code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added path normalization in buildKeystoneHTTPRoute (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_httproute.go:147-156): when gw.Path is non-empty but lacks a leading '/', it is prepended (e.g. 'identity' -> '/identity'); empty still falls back to defaultHTTPRoutePath. Added 'strings' import and a dedicated unit test TestBuildKeystoneHTTPRoute_PathWithoutLeadingSlash_Normalized in reconcile_httproute_test.go asserting the normalization.

code_comments.body:
**🚨 question (security):** Automatically allowing all pods in the Gateway namespace may weaken the intended isolation expressed by spec.networkPolicy.

When `spec.gateway` is set, this always adds an ingress peer for the entire Gateway namespace, even if `spec.networkPolicy` is intended to be more restrictive. In shared Gateway namespaces, this can grant broader access than the CR author expects. Consider guarding this with an explicit opt-in (e.g. a field under `spec.networkPolicy` or `spec.gateway`), or only adding this peer when no other ingress peers are defined, so that the CR’s `networkPolicy` remains the complete source of truth for ingress policy.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Behavior intentionally unchanged in buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go:115-129): a REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block was added explaining that REQ-008 mandates the namespace-wide peer so Gateway data-plane pods can reach Keystone when both spec.gateway and spec.networkPolicy are set, that user-specified peers remain the source of truth for all other ingress, and that pod-level selection would leak Gateway implementation into the operator while an opt-in field would alter REQ-008 semantics (deferred). The rationale is documented inline instead of altering the code.

resolution_summary: The two inline code comments were addressed directly: gateway path normalization now prepends a leading slash when missing (with a new test covering the case), and the Gateway-namespace ingress peer was kept intentionally, with the reviewer's concern documented in a REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT block citing REQ-008. The two overall comments were addressed by removing the silent hostname fallback in keystoneStatusEndpoint so misconfiguration surfaces loudly (also expanded in the doc comment), and by removing the leftover 'Reviewer: please verify.' note from the CC-0065 block in buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy (the now-present 'Reviewer: bitte prüfen.' is a new prompt on the disagreement rationale, not the original leftover). No additional files outside those directly referenced by the review were modified.
